metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: March of the Machine is a sluggish, value-heavy format containing more text than any previously printed set. The format emphasizes diverse strategies with powerful rares like Sunfall and Etali, Primal Conqueror serving as nearly unbeatable haymakers.
color_ranking: {G} Green — ramp engine for splashy decks
{R} Red — weakest by word of mouth

color_notes.Note: MOM is archetype-driven. Three main approaches dominate: aggro/tempo (with convoke and backup), midrange/ramp (green-based good stuff piles), and Dimir control. Multiverse Legends bonus sheet adds 65 reprints but many underperform due to lacking synergies.

key_gameplay_notes: Splashing is common — most decks lean on one of the set's two aggressive mechanics or the green ramp engine.
Convoke is the name of the game; bait opponent's convoke spells when possible.
Battles enable instant-speed blowouts with removal.
Multiverse Legends reprints often lack synergy — evaluate critically.

mechanics.name: Convoke
mechanics.summary: Tap creatures to help cast spells; each tapped creature pays one mana of its color.

mechanics.name: Backup
mechanics.summary: When this creature enters, choose another creature; it gains the backup ability until end of turn.

mechanics.name: Battles
mechanics.summary: New card type — double-faced permanents that require the opponent to defend until they flip into a permanent for you.
mechanics.performance: Aggressive battles excel in tempo decks; mediocre battles should be cut.

key_examples.name: Invasion of Fiora
key_examples.type_line: Battle — Siege // Legendary Creature — Human Noble

mechanics.name: Incubate
mechanics.summary: Create face-down 0/0 artifact creature tokens with N +1/+1 counters; flip them into Phyrexians by paying 2.
mechanics.performance: Solid value mechanic; pairs well with sacrifice synergies.

mechanics.name: Sacrifice Synergies
mechanics.summary: Rakdos engine — sacrifice cheap creatures for value.

mechanics.name: Graveyard / Mill
mechanics.summary: Dimir Control engine. Halo-Charged Skaab loops with Unseal the Necropolis or Ephara's Dispersal.

archetypes.name: Azorius Knights (Aggro/Tempo)
archetypes.strategy: Soft knights tribal with minimal payoffs. Builds as good stuff piles leveraging convoke.

archetypes.name: Dimir Graveyard (Control)
archetypes.strategy: Grindiest deck. Trades 1-for-1 and wins with remaining threats. Loops Halo-Charged Skaab with Unseal the Necropolis or Ephara's Dispersal.

key_cards.name: Aetherblade Agent
key_cards.type_line: Creature — Human Rogue // Creature — Phyrexian Rogue

archetypes.name: Rakdos Sacrifice (Aggro/Midrange)
archetypes.strategy: Play cheap creatures and sacrifice for value.

archetypes.name: Gruul Battles (Midrange)
archetypes.strategy: Maximize battle interactions. Tutor powerful battles and flip them with creatures. Splashes for bomb rares.

archetypes.name: Selesnya Counters (Aggro)
archetypes.strategy: Soft +1/+1 counter theme. Builds as aggressive deck or rampy green pile with white removal.

archetypes.name: Orzhov Phyrexians (Aggro/Midrange)
archetypes.strategy: Minimal Phyrexian payoffs; often plays as midrange leveraging black synergies.

archetypes.name: Izzet Convoke (Aggro/Tempo)
archetypes.strategy: Highest ceiling of all 2-color archetypes. Leverages small creatures with convoke payoffs.

archetypes.name: Boros Backup (Aggro)
archetypes.strategy: Backup mechanic synergies with aggressive positioning.

top_uncommons.name: Invasion of Amonkhet
top_uncommons.note: #1 — completely bonkers; one of the easiest 3-for-1s.

top_uncommons.name: Marshal of Zhalfir
top_uncommons.note: #3 — incredible rate for a lord with a tapping ability.

top_uncommons.name: Skyclave Aerialist
top_uncommons.type_line: Creature — Merfolk Scout // Creature — Phyrexian Merfolk Scout

multiverse_legends.description: Bonus sheet (mul) adding 65 reprinted legendary creatures from across Magic's history. Many underperform in MOM due to lacking synergy support.

cards_to_avoid.name: Dina, Soul Steeper
cards_to_avoid.note: Set lacks Pest and lifegain payoffs.

removal_priority.name: Deadly Derision
removal_priority.note: Premium rate; BB cost is the only drawback.

general_tips: Green midrange decks enable heavy splashing — prioritize lands and Skittering Surveyor.
Evaluate battles by flipping capability; cut mediocre battles from non-dedicated decks.
Convoke spells glue green ramp pieces together — Portent Tracker, Blighted Burgeoning, Overgrown Pest fit naturally.
Play around Cut Short, Volcanic Spite, and Angelic Intervention.
Red is rated as the weakest color — lean on convoke (Ral's Reinforcements) or use red as a support color.
Expect a diverse, complicated format — adapt strategies based on signals.
